open_known_gaps.summary: Client-side memory-exhaustion (PDF-bomb DoS) class — REDUCED this tick. The amplifier (viewer.js renderAll() supersede-guard race that let rapid zoom/fit-width accumulate duplicate page wrappers+canvases without bound) is FIXED and VERIFIED via TASK-316 (2026-06-09): renderAll() now re-checks token!==renderToken after `await getPage()` and again before appendChild, so stale renders bail without allocating a canvas or mutating the DOM (rapid-zoom repro settles 1/1, was 4/4). REMAINING gap: viewer.js loadDocument() still has no page-count cap and no processing timeout, so a crafted high-page-count PDF within the 50MB limit renders one canvas/page (+ one thumbnail/page) — now a LINEAR footprint, not a multiplied one. Client-side only, no server impact. Fix = developer task: add a numPages>1000 cap at the viewer.js load boundary. Documented in docs/security-guide.md.

notes: Clean review, no new vulnerabilities, no CRITICAL issues. Only code changed since last security run (20:35) is js/viewer.js — the TASK-316 renderAll() race fix (now VERIFIED). Audited the new guards: two token re-checks added (after `await getPage()` and before `appendChild`); a superseded render now bails before allocating any canvas backing store or touching the DOM. This CLOSES the SEC-002 amplifier (unbounded duplicate-canvas multiplication on rapid zoom) — verified by tester via chrome-devtools MCP (rapid-zoom repro settles 1/1, harsher burst 1/1, zero console errors). SEC-002 severity reduced accordingly: remaining gap is the load-boundary page-count cap in loadDocument() (still missing, still low — now a linear one-canvas-per-page footprint, not multiplied). upload.js validation boundary unchanged and correct (magic bytes %PDF-, MIME, .pdf ext, 0-byte reject, 50MB cap, sanitizeName strips \ / space - ). DOM-sink sweep across all js/: every innerHTML is a container clear (= ''), zero eval/Function/document.write/outerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML, zero fetch/XHR/.src, zero inline on* handlers; all file-supplied strings rendered via textContent. Live headers confirm full CSP + nosniff + X-Frame-Options + Referrer-Policy; .mjs MIME text/javascript; sensitive paths (.git 403, .bak 403, .md 403, .sh 403, ../ traversal 404) blocked; nginx -t valid; 0 failed SSH auths in 24h; no secrets in web JS (grep hits are renderToken/token false-positives). TLS still deferred until DNS cutover (expected). Stability gate: 0 SYSTEM CRITICAL open (TASK-316 now VERIFIED), 0 FAILED, 1 DONE-unverified — gate OPEN. No security blockers.
